explanation: This LTL property states that whenever the pilot is in control (standby) and the autopilot is currently in the NOMINAL state, in the next time step the autopilot will be in the STANDBY state.  This captures the requirement that the autopilot should change states from NOMINAL to STANDBY when the pilot is in control.

The LTL property captures the requirement that whenever the pilot is in control (standby), the autopilot will eventually transition from the NOMINAL state to the STANDBY state. It does this by expressing the implication that if the pilot is in control, then the autopilot will eventually transition to the STANDBY state. The chosen template uses the "until" operator to express this eventually transition.

This LTL property captures the requirement that whenever the autopilot is in NOMINAL mode and the pilot is in control (standby), the autopilot will transition to STANDBY mode in the next time step. The LTL property captures the requirement that whenever the pilot is in control (standby is true), the autopilot will eventually transition to the STANDBY state. The property uses the "until" operator (U) to ensure that the autopilot will eventually transition to the STANDBY state, and it uses the atomic proposition "standby" to represent the pilot being in control.
chosen_LTL_template: (((! (bool_exp1 & (X (! bool_exp1)))) U ((bool_exp1 & (X (! bool_exp1))) & (X ((G[0,N_DURATION] bool_exp3) | (G bool_exp3))))) | (G (! (bool_exp1 & (X (! bool_exp1))))))

explanation: The LTL formula captures the requirement that whenever the pilot is in control (standby is true), the autopilot will eventually transition to the STANDBY state. This is achieved by using the 'G' (globally) operator to ensure that the implication holds for all time steps. The antecedent of the implication checks for the pilot being in control (standby = true), and the consequent checks for the autopilot being in the STANDBY state. We use the 'F' (eventually) operator with a fixed duration of 1 to express that the STANDBY state must be reached within one time step after the pilot takes control.
